text: Yes, that's the problem on dividing zero by zero; it's not a valid concept. In Maths, that's called an 'indetermination'. When you're calculating limits (the value of an expression when the value of the parameter(s) tend to a concrete value), for example the expression 1/x, you can get zero -in this case, when x tends to infinite- or infinite -when x tends to zero. But in some cases you ca get 0/0. Since that makes no sense, you'll have to find a way to avoid that result.

text: I'm sorry, but it's actually worst :S If you divide any number (excepting zero and infinite) by an increasing number (you can try this out easily on a calculator), the result decreases, so when you divide by infinite, you get zero. If you divide by a decreasing number, the result increases, so when you divide by zero the result is infinite. But what happens when you divide zero by zero? Is the result zero or infinite? They're completely opposite concepts O.o
